Title: Create a new user
Preconditions: None
Test Data:
- Valid email: test@example.com
- Valid name: John Doe
- Valid password: Passw0rd!
Test Steps:
1. Call createUser("John Doe", "test@example.com", "Passw0rd!") method.
2. Verify the return value is true.
Expected Result: The createUser method returns true indicating successful creation of a new user.

Title: Check existing user credentials
Preconditions: A user with email test@example.com and password Passw0rd! has been created.
Test Steps:
1. Call checkUser("test@example.com", "Passw0rd!") method.
2. Verify the return value is true.
Expected Result: The checkUser method returns true indicating the user credentials are valid.

Title: Reset user password
Preconditions: A user with email test@example.com exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call resetPassword("test@example.com", "NewPassw0rd!") method.
2. Verify the return value is true.
Expected Result: The resetPassword method returns true indicating the password was successfully reset.

Title: Add devices to user account
Preconditions: A user with email test@example.com exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call addDevices("D123") method.
2. Verify the return value is true.
Expected Result: The addDevices method returns true indicating the device was successfully added to the user account.

Title: Retrieve user information
Preconditions: A user with email test@example.com exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call getUserInfo() method.
2. Verify the returned list contains the user's ID, name, and email.
Expected Result: The getUserInfo method returns a list containing the user's ID, name, and email.

Title: Get device information
Preconditions: A device with ID D123 exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call getDeviceInfo() method.
2. Verify the returned list contains the device's ID and title.
Expected Result: The getDeviceInfo method returns a list containing the device's ID and title.

Title: Set light configuration
Preconditions: A device with ID D123 exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call setLight(true) method.
2. Verify the return value is true.
Expected Result: The setLight method returns true indicating the light configuration was successfully set.

Preconditions: A device with ID D123 exists.

Title: Update history record
Preconditions: A device with ID D123 exists.
Test Data:
- Date: 2023-10-01
- Air humidity: 60.0
- Soil humidity: 40.0
- Air temperature: 25.0
- Luminosity: 75.0
Test Steps:
1. Call updateHistory("2023-10-01", 60.0, 40.0, 25.0) method.
2. Verify the return value is true.
Expected Result: The updateHistory method returns true indicating the history record was successfully updated.

Title: Retrieve history records
Preconditions: A device with ID D123 exists and has history records.
Test Steps:
1. Call getHistory() method.
2. Verify the returned list contains the date, air humidity, soil humidity, air temperature, and luminosity.
Expected Result: The getHistory method returns a list containing the history records.

Title: Retrieve alert message
Preconditions: An alert message exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call getMessage() method.
2. Verify the returned string contains the alert message.
Expected Result: The getMessage method returns the alert message.

Title: Access system and click "Serre"
Preconditions: System is accessible.
Test Steps:
1. Initiate access to the system.
2. Click the "Serre" button.
3. Verify the default message is displayed.
4. Check if there is at least one associated greenhouse.
5. If yes, display the list of greenhouses.
6. If no, display a default message.
Expected Result: The system displays the appropriate message based on the presence of associated greenhouses.

Title: Add devices with valid input
Preconditions: System is accessible.
Test Steps:
1. Initiate access to the system.
2. Click the "Add Devices" button.
3. Enter a valid device ID.
4. Verify the operation is executed successfully.
Expected Result: The system adds the device successfully.

Title: Add devices with invalid input
Preconditions: System is accessible.
Test Steps:
1. Initiate access to the system.
2. Click the "Add Devices" button.
3. Enter an invalid device ID.
4. Verify the operation fails and prompts for correction.
Expected Result: The system detects the invalid input and prompts for correction.

Title: Modify configuration with valid input
Preconditions: System is accessible and a greenhouse is connected.
Test Steps:
1. Initiate access to the system.
2. Connect to a greenhouse.
3. Click the "Configuration" button.
4. Enter valid configuration values.
5. Verify the operation is executed successfully.
Expected Result: The system modifies the configuration successfully.

Title: Modify configuration with invalid input
Preconditions: System is accessible and a greenhouse is connected.
Test Steps:
1. Initiate access to the system.
2. Connect to a greenhouse.
3. Click the "Configuration" button.
4. Enter invalid configuration values.
5. Verify the operation fails and prompts for correction.
Expected Result: The system detects the invalid input and prompts for correction.

Title: Loop back to configuration input after detecting value error
Preconditions: System is accessible and a greenhouse is connected.
Test Steps:
1. Initiate access to the system.
2. Connect to a greenhouse.
3. Click the "Configuration" button.
4. Enter invalid configuration values.
5. Verify the system detects the value error.
6. Prompt for correction.
7. Re-enter valid configuration values.
8. Verify the operation is executed successfully.
Expected Result: The system detects the initial invalid input, prompts for correction, and executes the operation successfully upon receiving valid input.

Title: Handle no associated greenhouses
Preconditions: System is accessible but no greenhouses are connected.
Test Steps:
1. Initiate access to the system.
2. Attempt to connect to a greenhouse.
3. Verify the system does not allow configuration changes due to no associated greenhouses.
Expected Result: The system prevents configuration changes due to the absence of associated greenhouses.

Title: Handle valid configuration input in loop
Preconditions: System is accessible and a greenhouse is connected.
Test Steps:
1. Initiate access to the system.
2. Connect to a greenhouse.
3. Click the "Configuration" button.
4. Enter valid configuration values.
5. Verify the operation is executed successfully.
6. Re-enter valid configuration values.
